description: Confounder: The model assumes only missing entries yield NA in comparisons, but comparing an

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In a pandas nullable integer (Int64) Series, comparing the Series to itself with `==` returns a Series of `pd.NA` values for missing entries (not `True`), because `pd.NA` uses three-valued logic — whereas in a float64 Series, missing entries compared to themselves yield `False` due to IEEE 754 NaN semantics.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes only missing entries yield NA in comparisons, but comparing any Int64 value (including non-missing ones) to `pd.NA` propagates NA for all elements, so result[0] is also `<NA>`, not `False`.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In a pandas nullable integer (Int64) Series, comparing the Series to itself with `==` returns a Series of `pd.NA` values for missing entries (not `True`), because `pd.NA` uses three-valued logic — whereas in a float64 Series, missing entries compared to themselves yield `False` due to IEEE 754 NaN semantics.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
